OSINTdefender The U.S. Army has officially named the new MV-75 Future Long Range Assault Aircraft (LRAA), the production version of Bell’s winning V-280 tiltrotor jet, Cheyenne II. The V-280 first flew in 2017 during demonstrations for the U.S. Army’s Future Vertical Lift program and its production version, the MV-75 Cheyenne II, is set to replace the UH-60 Black Hawk. tweet

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 Footage shows the large‑scale demolition of the central urban district (Casbah) of Bint Jbeil, southern Lebanon, using controlled charges. The IDF has seemingly established control over most of the city, including its outskirts and key positions. Bint Jbeil, long dubbed Hezbollah’s “Capital of the Resistance” and the site of Hassan Nasrallah’s 2000 “spider web” speech, has now seen its historic core levelled. @wfwitness

The vessel, bound for Malaysia, reportedly sank due to heavy winds, rough seas, and overcrowding after departing from southern Bangladesh. Nine people have been rescued so far. @wfwitness

Open Source Intel First crude tanker moves west through Hormuz since U.S. blockade The Malta flagged ‘VLCC Agios Fanourios’ is the first crude carrier to transit westbound through the Strait of Hormuz following the U.S. blockade on Iran’s ports. It aborted its first attempt on Sunday when US-Iran talks collapsed in Islamabad, then succeeded on its second try today, transiting along a Tehran-approved route between Qeshm and Larak islands. According to Iranian media, the ship is carrying 2 million barrels of oil. tweet

Status-6 (War & Military News) March 2026 the bloodiest month for Russia since the beginning of the full-scale invasion of Ukraine, British MoD says. The Russians reportedly suffered over 35,000 casualties, with 96% of these said to be caused by drones. BREAKING: Russian forces suffered more than 35,000 casualties in Ukraine in March - the highest rate of the war, the UK defence secretary has said. @JohnHealey_MP said 96% of those casualties were caused by drones. He also said the number of Russian casualties has been greater than the number of recruits for 4 straight months - Deborah Haynes tweet

⚡️ 🇬🇧 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 British Prime Minister Starmer reiterated that he won’t yield to pressure to join the U.S.’s conflict with Iran. "My position on the Iran war has been clear from the start. We're not going to get dragged into this war. It is not our war. And, a lot of pressure has been applied to me to take a different course, and that pressure included what happened last night. I'm not going to change my mind. I'm not going to yield. It is not in our national interest to join this war, and we will not do so. I know where I stand." @wfwitness

NEW: Satellite images show Iran using heavy equipment to dig out missile launchers trapped in underground tunnels during the ceasefire. Recent US intelligence estimates that roughly half of Iran’s missile launchers remain intact after a month of fighting. Many were likely buried by US and Israeli strikes on tunnel entrances, and Iran now appears to be recovering them. Source: CNN

Israeli Ambassador to the U.S. Yechiel Leiter on Lebanon talks: We certainly don’t want the French anywhere near these negotiations. We’d like to keep the French as far away as possible from pretty much everything. They’re not needed. They’re not a positive influence.

Behind the scenes, the situation is as follows - From Israeli media: Iran and the US continue to negotiate an agreement to end the war - especially as the US is currently imposing a naval blockade on the Strait of Hormuz and increasing pressure on the Iranians. However, Iran has climbed a high tree - and has publicly pledged several times that it will not agree to any agreement that does not also include a ceasefire in Lebanon - including a statement by a senior Iranian official on the subject just today. So far, the US has pressured Israel to stop the attacks deep inside Lebanon (Beirut and the Bekaa), and this has been the case for a week, but now - Iran is not satisfied with this, and is demanding a comprehensive ceasefire in Lebanon.

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Axios : U.S. and Iranian negotiators have made progress toward a framework agreement to end the war, two US officials said. A Pakistani delegation headed by army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir arrived in Tehran on Wednesday for talks. U.S. negotiators – including Vice President Vance, Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner – continued exchanging draft proposals with Iran via mediators. A new round of direct in‑person talks is likely in the coming days before the ceasefire expires on 21 April. U.S. officials said Trump’s naval blockade has cut off Iran’s oil exports, deepening its economic crisis and increasing pressure on Tehran. Iran’s internet shutdown, now in its 47th day, is costing the economy $50 million daily. @wfwitness
